observation: I can see I'm on a country selection page for Deriv. The dropdown country selector is open and shows a comprehensive list of countries with flags and names. I can see many countries starting from Afghanistan all the way to Zimbabwe. I can see Australia (🇦🇺) in the list at element 71. Since the previous actions successfully selected Australia, I need to tap on it to select it as my residence.
reasoning: As a 70-year-old user, I can see the country dropdown is already open showing many country options. I can clearly see Australia in the list with its flag. I need to select Australia as my residence to continue with the signup process.
ux_issues: The country list is very long and requires scrolling, which might be difficult for elderly users
The text size appears adequate but the long list could be overwhelming

dual_diagnosis.visual_observation: The dropdown menu that should have appeared with country options including Australia is not visible in the AFTER screenshot. The interface remains unchanged with only the 'Select' placeholder text still showing in the dropdown field.
dual_diagnosis.diagnosis: The tap action on the dropdown did not trigger the expected country selection menu to appear. This could be due to JavaScript event handler not firing, dropdown component not being properly initialized, or the tap target area being incorrectly defined.
